psalm-74-1.verse: O God, why have You rejected us forever? Why does Your anger smoke against the sheep of Your pasture?
psalm-74-1.prayer: Lord, when I feel forgotten or distant from Your presence, help me remember that You are still faithful and near to Your people.

psalm-74-2.verse: Remember Your congregation, which You have purchased of old, Which You have redeemed to be the tribe of Your inheritance; And this Mount Zion, where You have dwelt.
psalm-74-2.prayer: Father, remember Your people with mercy. Thank You for redeeming us and calling us to belong to You.

psalm-74-3.verse: Turn Your footsteps toward the perpetual ruins; The enemy has damaged everything within the sanctuary.
psalm-74-3.prayer: Lord, restore what has been broken and bring healing where there has been loss, damage, or discouragement.

psalm-74-4.verse: Your adversaries have roared in the midst of Your meeting place; They have set up their own standards for signs.
psalm-74-4.prayer: Father, when voices of opposition seem loud, help me stay anchored in Your truth and confidence in Your power.

psalm-74-5.verse: It seems as if one had lifted up His axe in a forest of trees.
psalm-74-5.prayer: Lord, protect what is holy and precious. Help me honor the things that belong to You with reverence and care.

psalm-74-6.verse: And now all its carved work They smash with hatchet and hammers.
psalm-74-6.prayer: Father, defend the work of Your hands and preserve what You have built in my life.

psalm-74-7.verse: They have burned Your sanctuary to the ground; They have defiled the dwelling place of Your name.
psalm-74-7.prayer: Lord, when sacred things are treated carelessly, give me a heart that values Your presence, Your Word, and Your name.

psalm-74-8.verse: They said in their heart, “Let us completely subdue them.” They have burned all the meeting places of God in the land.
psalm-74-8.prayer: Father, protect Your people and places of worship. Let Your truth remain strong through every generation.

psalm-74-9.verse: We do not see our signs; There is no longer any prophet, Nor is there any among us who knows how long.
psalm-74-9.prayer: Lord, when I cannot clearly see signs of Your work, help me trust that You are still moving even in silence.

psalm-74-10.verse: How long, O God, will the adversary revile, And the enemy spurn Your name forever?
psalm-74-10.prayer: Father, when I wonder how long hardship will continue, strengthen my patience and renew my hope in You.

psalm-74-11.verse: Why do You withdraw Your hand, even Your right hand? From within Your bosom, destroy them!
psalm-74-11.prayer: Lord, do not hold back Your help. Stretch out Your hand in mercy and bring deliverance according to Your will.

psalm-74-12.verse: Yet God is my king from of old, Who works deeds of deliverance in the midst of the earth.
psalm-74-12.prayer: Father, thank You that You have been my King from long ago. Help me remember Your saving works and trust Your authority.

psalm-74-13.verse: You divided the sea by Your strength; You broke the heads of the sea monsters in the waters.
psalm-74-13.prayer: Lord, You rule over the sea and every force of chaos. Speak peace over every storm in my life.

psalm-74-14.verse: You crushed the heads of Leviathan; You gave him as food for the creatures of the wilderness.
psalm-74-14.prayer: Father, You overcome every enemy and every power that rises against Your purposes. Help me trust Your victory.

psalm-74-15.verse: You broke open springs and torrents; You dried up ever-flowing streams.
psalm-74-15.prayer: Lord, You open springs and dry up rivers. Remind me that You can provide, redirect, and make a way where I see none.

psalm-74-16.verse: Yours is the day, Yours also is the night; You have prepared the light and the sun.
psalm-74-16.prayer: Father, the day and night belong to You. Help me trust Your care in both bright seasons and dark ones.

psalm-74-17.verse: You have established all the boundaries of the earth; You have made summer and winter.
psalm-74-17.prayer: Lord, You established the boundaries of the earth and formed the seasons. Help me rest in Your wisdom and order.

psalm-74-18.verse: Remember this, O Lord, that the enemy has reviled, And a foolish people has spurned Your name.
psalm-74-18.prayer: Father, when people mock Your name or reject Your truth, help me remain faithful, humble, and devoted to You.

psalm-74-19.verse: Do not deliver the soul of Your turtledove to the wild beast; Do not forget the life of Your afflicted forever.
psalm-74-19.prayer: Lord, protect the vulnerable and do not forget those who depend on You. Keep my soul safe in Your care.

psalm-74-20.verse: Consider the covenant; For the dark places of the land are full of the habitations of violence.
psalm-74-20.prayer: Father, remember Your covenant and shine Your light in dark places. Let Your faithfulness bring hope where there is fear.

psalm-74-21.verse: Let not the oppressed return dishonored; Let the afflicted and needy praise Your name.
psalm-74-21.prayer: Lord, do not let the oppressed turn away in shame. Let the poor and needy praise Your name through Your mercy and help.

psalm-74-22.verse: Arise, O God, and plead Your own cause; Remember how the foolish man reproaches You all day long.
psalm-74-22.prayer: Father, arise and defend Your cause. Help me trust that Your truth, justice, and glory will prevail.

psalm-74-23.verse: Do not forget the voice of Your adversaries, The uproar of those who rise against You which ascends continually.
psalm-74-23.prayer: Lord, do not forget the noise of opposition. Silence what dishonors You and let Your name be exalted above all.

psalm-74-closing.verse:
Lord, thank You for being King over every season, every storm, and every struggle. When I feel confused, forgotten, or surrounded by opposition, help me remember Your covenant, Your power, and Your faithfulness. Restore what is broken, defend what belongs to You, and keep my heart anchored in hope. May my life trust Your timing, honor Your name, and praise You through every season.

In Jesus’ name, Amen.
psalm-74-closing.prayer:
Lord, thank You for being King over every season, every storm, and every struggle. When I feel confused, forgotten, or surrounded by opposition, help me remember Your covenant, Your power, and Your faithfulness. Restore what is broken, defend what belongs to You, and keep my heart anchored in hope. May my life trust Your timing, honor Your name, and praise You through every season.

In Jesus’ name, Amen.
